name: Romanillos de Medinaceli — circular por La Maltida
region_intro: Hiking around Baraona, located in Soria, Spain, offers routes through rural landscapes and gentle terrain. The area is characterized by rolling hills, agricultural fields, and paths that often follow river valleys. Elevation changes are generally moderate, making the trails accessible for various skill levels. Best hiking trails around Baraona The most popular hiking route is Yellow from Miño de Medinaceli — circular, a 5.9 miles (9.6 km) trail that takes 2 hours 32 minutes to complete, featuring rural paths around Miño de Medinaceli. Another top favourite among local hikers is Miño de Medinaceli — circular via the Erm, a moderate 6.7 miles (10.9 km) path. This route offers varied terrain, including sections near the Ermita. Local hikers also love the Fuentegelmes - Torete River Loop, a 6.7 miles (10.8 km) trail leading through river valleys and open countryside, often completed in about 2 hours 57 minutes. Hiking around Baraona is defined by rural paths, river valleys, and rolling hills. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to moderate hikes. The routes in Baraona are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 1 review. More than 7 hikers have used komoot to explore Baraona's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when hiking around Baraona?
A: Hiking around Baraona, Soria, Spain, primarily features rural paths, rolling hills, and agricultural fields. Many routes follow river valleys, offering a mix of open countryside and natural landscapes. The elevation changes are generally moderate, making the trails accessible for various skill levels.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Baraona?
A: Yes, Baraona offers several circular routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1263738098/yellow-from-mino-de-medinaceli-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Yellow from Miño de Medinaceli — circular&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 9.6 km long. Another well-liked circular path is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1263338437/mino-de-medinaceli-circular-via-the-erm" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Miño de Medinaceli — circular via the Erm&lt;/a&gt;, covering approximately 10.9 km with varied terrain.

Q: What are some interesting landmarks or places to see along the trails in Baraona?
A: Hikers can discover several points of interest. You might encounter the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2677132" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rello Walled Village&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2682869" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruins of Miño de Medinaceli&lt;/a&gt;, or the charming &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2682856" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Village Fountain&lt;/a&gt;. The area also features unique attractions like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7169482" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Picasso mural paintings&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: How many hiking trails are available in the Baraona area?
A: There are over 9 hiking trails documented around Baraona, offering a good selection for different preferences and abilities.

Q: What is the general difficulty level of hiking trails in Baraona?
A: The trails in Baraona are generally considered easy to moderate. Out of the available routes, 4 are rated as easy and 5 as moderate, making the area suitable for a wide range of hikers, from beginners to those looking for a slightly more challenging walk.

Q: Are there any river walks or trails that follow water in Baraona?
A: Yes, many routes in Baraona follow river valleys. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1263525850/fuentegelmes-torete-river-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuentegelmes - Torete River Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a notable trail that leads through river valleys and open countryside, offering scenic views of the local waterways.

Q: What do other hikers say about their experience in Baraona?
A: The hiking routes in Baraona are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 1 review. Hikers often praise the peaceful rural paths and the accessible nature of the trails.

Q: What is a good moderate hike in Baraona?
A: For a moderate hike, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1263689905/romanillos-de-medinaceli-circular-por-la-maltida" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Romanillos de Medinaceli — circular por La Maltida&lt;/a&gt;. This route is approximately 11.5 km long and offers a good balance of distance and moderate elevation changes through the local landscape.

Q: Are there any trails suitable for a longer day hike in Baraona?
A: For a longer day hike, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1263525850/fuentegelmes-torete-river-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuentegelmes - Torete River Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a good option, covering about 10.8 km. Another choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1263338437/mino-de-medinaceli-circular-via-the-erm" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Miño de Medinaceli — circular via the Erm&lt;/a&gt;, which is approximately 10.9 km.

Q: What kind of natural features can I expect to see while hiking in Baraona?
A: Hikers in Baraona will primarily experience landscapes characterized by rolling hills, agricultural fields, and river valleys. The region offers a tranquil rural setting with open countryside views.

Q: Is Baraona a good destination for hikers looking for less strenuous routes?
A: Yes, Baraona is well-suited for hikers seeking less strenuous routes. Many trails are rated as easy or moderate, with gentle terrain and accessible paths through rural landscapes, making it ideal for relaxed walks.
tips: Commemorative crosses to the fallen on the outskirts of Romanillos de Medinaceli. This is a point where the off-road trail begins.

Romanillos de Medinaceli is a small and charming town in the Altos de Barahona region. Located next to an old Roman road. La Cañada Oriental Soriana crosses the town, as well as one of the GR86 routes, which makes it a central point for hiking trails.

waypoints.name: Stone Crosses and Ruins of Romanillos de Medinaceli
